display.purpose: Home hero H1 only. vmin-scaled, never used elsewhere.

headline.purpose: Page H1s and major section H2s.

motion.name: card-hover-lift
motion.purpose: MotionCard hover lift, paired with border-color shift to accent.

motion.name: nav-scroll-transition
motion.purpose: Nav background/blur crossfade on scroll threshold. No shadow.

motion.name: reveal-in
motion.purpose: Scroll-driven section reveal (CSS-only, no JS observer), translateY(22px)+opacity.

motion.name: page-fade
motion.purpose: Page-level entrance fade on route change.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default CTA. Electric Blue fill, sharp corners, darkens on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-block; padding: 14px 28px; background: #0052ff; color: #fff; border: 1px solid #0052ff; border-radius: 0; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; transition: background-color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0040cc; border-color: #0f172a; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0052ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: White-fill button for lower-emphasis actions on light backgrounds.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-block; padding: 14px 28px; background: #fff; color: #0f172a; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 0; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #00d1ff; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0052ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Frosted button for use over dark hero imagery only. The single deliberate glassmorphism use in the system.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-block; padding: 14px 28px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); color: #fff; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); border-radius: 0; font-weight: 500; backdrop-filter: blur(4px); transition: background-color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.2); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat card with border-color hover shift and a subtle transform lift. No shadow, ever.
components.html:
Nuclear Analytics

EDIM is a physics-based framework that identifies and corrects calculational bias in predictive models.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 0; padding: 24px; transition: border-color 0.2s, transform 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: #00d1ff; transform: translateY(-4px); } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.125rem; color: #0f172a; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-card-body { margin-top: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #475569; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Fixed header. Transparent-to-solid crossfade on the home hero (backdrop-blur + bg-opacity once scrolled, no shadow); solid white everywhere else.
components.html: CORE DEFENSESMissionSolutions
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 36px; padding: 16px 24px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid #e2e8f0; } .ds-nav-logo { font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; color: #0f172a; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-nav-link { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #475569; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; padding-bottom: 4px; transition: color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #0f172a; border-color: #00d1ff; } .ds-nav-link--active { color: #0052ff; border-color: #0052ff; font-weight: 500; }

narrative.overview: Core Defenses reads like the interface of a precision instrument, not a startup's marketing site. The original brief called for a dark, stealth-ops mood in the register of Palantir or Anduril; the team deliberately rejected that in favor of a bright, high-contrast surface built for extended technical reading. The audience is security analysts and infrastructure engineers evaluating whether EDIM and ENADOX are credible for a nuclear plant, a defense program, or a utility grid, not consumers being sold excitement. Every element earns its place functionally: a well-lit lab bench, not a movie set. The system explicitly rejects AI-marketing visual and verbal clichés: gradient text, glassmorphism, hero-metric templates, tiny uppercase eyebrows above every section, drop-shadow lift cards, and copy that sounds inspirational rather than factual. It also rejects the dark stealth-ops aesthetic considered and abandoned in the original brief.
keyCharacteristics: Sharp corners everywhere (0-radius) — rigidity and precision over friendliness
Depth via 1px border-color shift, never a shadow
Electric Blue + Sentinel Cyan on a white/cool-slate surface, not navy-and-black
Copy is factual, short-sentence, engineer-written — no philosophy, no flowery adjectives
Motion is restrained and functional: scroll reveals, hover micro-interactions, nothing choreographed

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Electric Blue is the only saturated color used for interactive commitment (buttons, active states). Sentinel Cyan is a highlight, never a CTA fill.

rules.name: The No-Flourish Rule
rules.body: No italics, no serif accents, no display-font body copy. One display face, one body face, nothing decorative layered on top.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is prohibited as a depth cue anywhere in the system, including the nav. Focus rings use a 2px solid outline, never a shadow-based glow.

dos: Do keep every corner at 0-radius — cards, buttons, images, badges.
Do use a 1px border-color shift (never a shadow) to communicate hover/focus/active depth.
Do size hero-scale type and spacing with vmin-based clamp() so short viewports compress proportionally.
Do write copy the way ams-corp.com does: short sentences, real technical facts, no meaningless adjectives.
Do gate every animation on prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference, with content visible-by-default.
donts: Don't use box-shadow as a depth/elevation cue anywhere in the system, including the nav.
Don't write mission-critical, cutting-edge, world-class, redefining, or other flowery AI-marketing adjectives.
Don't write philosophy-voiced copy (we believe…, our philosophy…) or contrast-aphorisms (measurement over assumption).
Don't add gradient text, glassmorphism-as-default, hero-metric templates, or uppercase eyebrow labels above every section.
Don't patch short-viewport overlap with a fixed arbitrary top-padding value — use the nav-height-derived offset plus vmin-scaled hero content.
